g_opus.cited_a: Prepaid program dues we have collected sit as an obligation until the coverage period is delivered.
g_opus.cited_b: Ratable recognition posts each month of the plan.
g_opus.reason: Different OBJECT and causal position. Side A ("deferred_membership_fees") is a balance-sheet liability -- prepaid dues sitting "as an obligation until the coverage period is delivered." Side B ("membership_fee_income") is recognized income -- "ratable recognition posts each month." One is the unearned obligation (a stock/liability); the other is the revenue released from it (a flow/income). These occupy different causal positions (upstream deferral vs downstream recognition), so MECHANISM differs. Default-DIFFERENT governs.

g_sonnet_a.cited_a: Prepaid program dues we have collected sit as an obligation until the coverage period is delivered.
g_sonnet_a.cited_b: Ratable recognition posts each month of the plan.
g_sonnet_a.reason: Different OBJECT and MECHANISM. Side A's object is the deferred-fee liability balance — dues collected up front that sit as an unearned obligation until service is delivered (the deferral/build side). Side B's object is membership_fee_income — the recognized revenue amount that posts to the income statement each month (the recognition/release side). These are two distinct financial-statement line items (balance-sheet liability vs. P&L income), not the same metric, and the causal mechanisms are opposite: A is driven by new cash collections creating an obligation, B is driven by the passage of time/ratable amortization converting that obligation into income. Related by the same underlying membership program, but not the same object or mechanism, so default-DIFFERENT stands.

g_sonnet_b.cited_a: Prepaid program dues we have collected sit as an obligation until the coverage period is delivered.
g_sonnet_b.cited_b: Ratable recognition posts each month of the plan.
g_sonnet_b.reason: Side A describes the deferred (unearned) membership fee liability sitting on the balance sheet until coverage is delivered -- the OBJECT is an obligation/balance. Side B describes the monthly ratable recognition of membership fee income into revenue -- the OBJECT is recognized income. These are different quantities occupying different causal/accounting positions (liability roll-off vs income statement recognition), not the same measured object, so MECHANISM/OBJECT sameness is not established. Default-DIFFERENT governs.
